text.title: AI Feature Eval Set Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team's eval is 5 hand-picked examples. Every prompt tweak claims a 'win' that doesn't reproduce in production.
antipattern.root-cause: Below 30 gold + 10 adversarial rows, statistical noise dominates real effects.
antipattern.fix: Grow eval set to spec minimums; rebaseline before declaring any prompt-change win.

antipattern.symptom: Judge prompt is 'rate output 1-5'. Same input scored 2 / 4 / 5 across re-runs.
antipattern.root-cause: Free-form judges have high inter-rater noise; without structured rubric the score is non-reproducible.
antipattern.fix: Replace with structured Pydantic rubric (evidence list + boolean + failure_modes); re-run eval — noise drops > 3x.

antipattern.symptom: Eval set unchanged for 8 months while production input distribution shifted.
antipattern.root-cause: No drift-watch rows; old eval scores well even as users see degraded quality.
antipattern.fix: Add monthly drift-watch refresh; sample anonymized production traffic.

antipattern.symptom: PR claims +4pp accuracy. Reviewer notices eval-set version bumped in same PR — gain is from row swap, not prompt change.
antipattern.root-cause: Comparing across eval-set versions without baseline split is invalid.
antipattern.fix: CI rejects PRs that bump eval version + claim a score delta in the same commit. Re-baseline first.
